concept_name: Magnetic Force on a Current-Carrying Wire (F = I L × B)
source_book: DC Pandey Vol 2 Ch.36 (force on a current-carrying conductor in a magnetic field) — table-of-contents reference only; teaching authored from first principles. Concept A15, the natural successor to magnetic_force_moving_charge (the prerequisite): a wire is just a pipe full of moving charges, so the single-charge force F = qv×B summed over all the carriers in a length L becomes F = I L × B. The atomic idea being taught is (1) the DERIVATION as a picture — n carriers per unit volume, each drifting at v_d, the per-carrier force qv_dB adds up to (nAL)·q·v_d·B = (I)(LB), so the messy microscopic picture collapses into one clean macroscopic law; (2) DIRECTION via the right-hand rule on the macroscopic vectors L (along conventional current) and B; (3) the sin θ between L and B — the wire-orientation analogue of the v–B angle; (4) two classic traps — the 30° decoy angle (θ is between L and B, not between the wire and the page edge) and the bent-wire result (only the straight chord from end to end matters, F = I (L_chord) × B); (5) the closed-loop net-zero result that seeds the next concept (torque_on_current_loop_in_field). RULE 31 CONVERSION (2026-07-02): restructured from 7 Socratic states into 6 distinct-motion beats — hook+direction merged (hand phases + authored current flip), derivation kept as the aha, the angle trap becomes a 16a contrast beat with the θ slider LIVE (per-state contextual control), chord rule, loop→torque, then explore with all controls. Indian anchor: the electric motor in every Indian home — the ceiling fan, the mixer-grinder, the borewell pump — every one of them spins because F = I L × B pushes on current-carrying wires sitting in a magnetic field.

real_world_anchor.primary: Look up at the ceiling fan spinning over your head right now. Inside its motor, coils of copper wire carry current, and they sit inside the magnetic field of fixed magnets. The field pushes on every current-carrying wire with a force F = I L × B, and those pushes add up into the turning that spins the blades. The same force runs the mixer-grinder in the kitchen and the borewell pump that lifts water — every electric motor in an Indian home is F = I L × B at work.
real_world_anchor.secondary: Picture the loudspeaker in your phone or in a cheap Bluetooth speaker. A coil of wire is glued to the paper cone and sits in the gap of a small permanent magnet. When the music signal sends current back and forth through the coil, F = I L × B pushes the coil — and the cone — in and out hundreds of times a second. That push is the sound you hear. The coil is a current-carrying wire in a magnetic field; the force is exactly the one in this lesson.
real_world_anchor.tertiary: Picture an electric local train or the Delhi Metro pulling out of a station. Its traction motors are huge versions of the ceiling-fan motor — thick current-carrying windings sitting in a strong magnetic field. F = I L × B on those windings is what accelerates a hundred-tonne train. Scale up the current and the field, and the same little formula moves a whole train.

L.name: Length of wire inside the field

B.name: Magnitude of the uniform magnetic field

current_dir.name: Direction of conventional current along the wire

n.name: Carrier number density (derivation only — shown as in-canvas math, not a slider)
n.derived: carrier density of copper, ~8.5e28; used only in the STATE_2 derivation picture

A.name: Wire cross-sectional area (derivation only — shown as in-canvas math, not a slider)
A.derived: cross-section of the wire; cancels in I = nAqv_d during the STATE_2 derivation

q.name: Charge per carrier (derivation only — shown as in-canvas math, not a slider)
q.derived: electron charge magnitude; folds into I = nAqv_d during the STATE_2 derivation

v_d.name: Drift velocity of carriers (derivation only — shown as in-canvas math, not a slider)
v_d.derived: carrier drift speed; folds into I = nAqv_d during the STATE_2 derivation

formulas.force_vector: F = I (L × B) — L is a vector of magnitude (wire length) pointing along the conventional current; the cross product gives the direction (right-hand rule).
formulas.force_magnitude: F = B I L sin θ — maximum when L ⊥ B (θ = 90°), zero when L ∥ B (θ = 0° or 180°).
formulas.derivation_from_single_charge: Each carrier feels q v_d B. A length L of wire holds nAL carriers. Total F = (nAL)(q v_d B) = (n A q v_d)(L B) = I L B, since I = n A q v_d.
formulas.bent_wire_chord_rule: For a wire bent between two end points, only the straight chord from start to end matters: F = I (L_chord × B). The bends cancel.
formulas.closed_loop_net_force: For a closed loop in a uniform field, the chord from start to end is zero, so the net force is zero — but opposite sides feel opposite forces, which produces a torque (next concept).

constraints: B must be uniform across the wire — a non-uniform field changes the force along the length.
L is a vector along the conventional current direction; reversing the current reverses F.
θ is the angle between L (the current direction) and B — NOT the angle the wire makes with the page edge or any drawn reference line.
When L ∥ B (θ = 0°), the cross product is zero — the wire feels no force however large the current.
For a bent or curved wire in a uniform field, F depends only on the straight chord joining the two ends: F = I (L_chord × B).
For a closed loop in a uniform field the net force is exactly zero (the chord closes on itself); the loop feels a torque instead.

aha_moment.statement: A wire is just moving charges; summing qv×B over them gives F=ILB.
aha_moment.visual_confirmation: In STATE_2 the nAL carriers each carry a tiny qv_dB arrow; they stack into one big F arrow, and the symbols collapse: nAqv_d is exactly the current I, leaving F = I L B.

STATE_1.title: The Push — and which way it points

scene_composition.text: Why does an electric motor turn?

Blue arrows = uniform field B.
Orange wire carries current I.
L points along the current.

Right hand:
fingers along L (the current),
curl toward B, thumb gives F.
Reverse the current → the force reverses. Ceiling fan, mixer, pump — every motor runs on this one push.

tts_sentences.text_en: Look up at the ceiling fan turning over your head. Inside its motor, copper wires carry current inside a magnetic field, and the field pushes on each wire.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अपने सिर के ऊपर घूमता ceiling fan देखिए। इसके motor के अंदर, copper wires एक magnetic field में current ले जाती हैं, और field हर wire पर push करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ తల పైన తిరుగుతున్న ceiling fan చూడండి. దాని motor లోపల, copper wires ఒక magnetic field లో current తీసుకెళ్తాయి, మరియు field ప్రతి wire ని push చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: The blue arrows are the field B. The orange wire carries current I along the length L. That push is F equals I times L cross B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: blue arrows field B हैं। orange wire, length L के साथ current I ले जाती है। वह push है F equals I times L cross B।
tts_sentences.text_te: blue arrows field B. orange wire, length L వెంట current I తీసుకెళ్తుంది. ఆ push అంటే F equals I times L cross B.

tts_sentences.text_en: Which way? Use the right hand. Point your fingers along L, the current direction.
tts_sentences.text_hi: किस direction में? right hand इस्तेमाल करिए। अपनी उंगलियाँ L की तरफ point करिए, current direction में।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఏ direction లో? right hand వాడండి. మీ వేళ్ళను L వైపు point చేయండి, current direction లో.

tts_sentences.text_en: Curl them toward B, the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उन्हें B, यानी field की तरफ curl करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: వాటిని B, అంటే field వైపు curl చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your thumb now points along F — the force on the wire, perpendicular to both the wire and the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आपका thumb अब F के साथ point करता है — wire पर force, wire और field दोनों के perpendicular।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ thumb ఇప్పుడు F వెంట point చేస్తుంది — wire పై force, wire మరియు field రెండింటికీ perpendicular.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch the current flip the other way — the force flips with it. That sign change is how a motor keeps turning the same way.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए current दूसरी दिशा में flip होता है — force भी इसके साथ flip होती है। वही sign change है जिससे motor एक ही direction में घूमती रहती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: current వేరే వైపు flip అవడం చూడండి — force కూడా దానితో పాటు flip అవుతుంది. ఆ sign change వల్లనే motor ఒకే direction లో తిరుగుతూ ఉంటుంది.

STATE_2.title: Derivation as a picture: add up qv×B over every carrier → F = I L × B

scene_composition.text: Where does F = I L × B come from?

A wire is a pipe of moving charges.
Each carrier feels q v_d B.
A length L holds n·A·L carriers.

F = (n·A·L)·(q·v_d·B)
     = (n·A·q·v_d)·(L·B)
and n·A·q·v_d is just I.
So F = I L B. The messy carrier picture collapses into one clean law. A wire is just charges — qv×B, added up.

tts_sentences.text_en: You already know the force on one moving charge: q times v d cross B. A wire is just a pipe full of these charges, drifting at the drift speed v d.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आप पहले से जानते हैं एक moving charge पर force: q times v d cross B। एक wire बस इन्हीं charges से भरा pipe है, drift speed v d पर drift करते हुए।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీకు ఇప్పటికే ఒక moving charge పై force తెలుసు: q times v d cross B. ఒక wire అంటే ఈ charges తో నిండిన pipe మాత్రమే, drift speed v d తో drift అవుతూ.

tts_sentences.text_en: A length L of wire holds n times A times L carriers, and each one feels q v d B — so the total force is n A L times q v d B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: wire की length L में n times A times L carriers होती हैं, और हर एक q v d B feel करती है — तो total force है n A L times q v d B।
tts_sentences.text_te: wire లో length L లో n times A times L carriers ఉంటాయి, ప్రతి ఒక్కటి q v d B feel చేస్తుంది — కాబట్టి total force n A L times q v d B.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now regroup: n times A times q times v d is exactly the current I. So the whole thing collapses to F equals I L B.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब regroup करिए: n times A times q times v d बिल्कुल current I है। तो पूरी चीज़ F equals I L B में collapse हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు regroup చేయండి: n times A times q times v d సరిగ్గా current I. కాబట్టి మొత్తం విషయం F equals I L B గా collapse అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That is the big idea. A wire is just charges, and I L cross B is q v cross B added up over all of them.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यही बड़ा idea है। एक wire बस charges है, और I L cross B वही q v cross B है जो उन सभी पर add हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇదే పెద్ద idea. ఒక wire కేవలం charges మాత్రమే, మరియు I L cross B అంటే q v cross B వాటన్నింటి మీద కలిపినది.

STATE_3.title: The angle trap: θ is between L and B — drag it and watch F

scene_composition.text: What is the angle θ in F = B I L sin θ?

Tempting wrong answer: 30°,
the angle the wire makes
with the edge of the page.

True θ = angle between L and B.
Here L ⊥ B, so θ = 90°
and F = B I L (maximum).
Always read θ between the current direction L and the field B — never between the wire and some drawn line. Drag θ toward 0 and F shrinks as sin θ.

tts_sentences.text_en: The magnitude is B times I times L times sine theta. Everything hangs on which angle theta is.
tts_sentences.text_hi: magnitude है B times I times L times sine theta। सब कुछ इस पर निर्भर है कि angle theta कौन सा है।
tts_sentences.text_te: magnitude అనేది B times I times L times sine theta. అన్నీ ఏ angle theta అనే దానిపై ఆధారపడి ఉంటాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The tempting answer is thirty degrees — the angle the wire makes with the page edge. It is a trap. That angle has nothing to do with the physics.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आकर्षक जवाब है thirty degrees — वह angle जो wire, page edge से बनाती है। यह एक trap है। उस angle का physics से कोई लेना-देना नहीं है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆకర్షణీయమైన జవాబు thirty degrees — wire, page edge తో చేసే angle. ఇది ఒక trap. ఆ angle కి physics తో ఎటువంటి సంబంధం లేదు.

tts_sentences.text_en: Theta is the angle between L, the current direction, and B, the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Theta, L यानी current direction और B यानी field के बीच का angle है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Theta అనేది L అంటే current direction మరియు B అంటే field మధ్య ఉన్న angle.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here the length L points across the field, so theta is ninety degrees, and the force is at its maximum, B I L.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ length L field के आर-पार point करता है, इसलिए theta ninety degrees है, और force अपने maximum B I L पर है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ length L field అడ్డంగా point చేస్తుంది, కాబట్టి theta ninety degrees, మరియు force దాని maximum B I L వద్ద ఉంటుంది.
tts_sentences.math_show: F = B I L\,\sin 90^{\circ} = B I L

tts_sentences.text_en: Now drag the angle slider. Tilt L toward B and the force falls off as sine theta; line L up with B and it drops to nothing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब angle slider को drag करिए। L को B की तरफ tilt करिए और force sine theta के अनुसार गिरती है; L को B के साथ line up करिए और यह शून्य हो जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు angle slider ని drag చేయండి. L ని B వైపు tilt చేయండి — force sine theta తో పడిపోతుంది; L ని B తో line up చేయండి — అది సున్నాకి తగ్గుతుంది.

misconception_watch.belief: θ in F = BIL sin θ is the angle the wire makes with the edge of the page or with the horizontal.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: θ is ALWAYS the angle between the current direction L and the field B. The angle to any drawn line on the page is irrelevant.

STATE_4.title: Bent wire = its straight chord: F = I (L_chord × B)

scene_composition.text: A bent wire — does every kink matter?

The wire zig-zags between two ends.
It looks complicated —
but the bends cancel.

Only the straight CHORD
from one end to the other counts.
F = I (L_chord × B).
In a uniform field the bends cancel; the force depends only on the straight line joining the two ends. Replace any bent wire with its chord.

tts_sentences.text_en: Here is a wire that zig-zags between two end points. It carries the same current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: यहाँ एक wire है जो दो end points के बीच zig-zag करती है। यह वही current I ले जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇక్కడ రెండు end points మధ్య zig-zag చేసే ఒక wire ఉంది. ఇది అదే current I తీసుకెళ్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: It looks like we should add up the force on every twist and bend. But in a uniform field, all the bends cancel out.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लगता है हमें हर twist और bend पर force add करनी चाहिए। लेकिन uniform field में, सारे bends cancel out हो जाते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: ప్రతి twist మరియు bend పై force ని add చేయాలనిపిస్తుంది. కానీ uniform field లో, అన్ని bends cancel out అవుతాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Only the straight chord from one end to the other matters.
tts_sentences.text_hi: केवल एक end से दूसरे end तक straight chord ही matter करती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఒక end నుండి మరొక end వరకు straight chord మాత్రమే matter చేస్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Watch — replace the whole bent wire with that single straight chord.
tts_sentences.text_hi: देखिए — पूरी bent wire को उस single straight chord से replace करिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: చూడండి — మొత్తం bent wire ని ఆ single straight chord తో replace చేయండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: The force is I times that chord vector cross B. The wiggles in between contribute nothing.
tts_sentences.text_hi: force है I times वह chord vector cross B। बीच में जो wiggles हैं वे कुछ contribute नहीं करते।
tts_sentences.text_te: force అంటే I times ఆ chord vector cross B. మధ్యలో ఉన్న wiggles ఏమీ contribute చేయవు.

tts_sentences.text_en: So for any bent or curved wire in a uniform field, just draw the straight line joining its two ends.
tts_sentences.text_hi: तो uniform field में किसी भी bent या curved wire के लिए, बस उसके दोनों ends को जोड़ने वाली straight line खींचिए।
tts_sentences.text_te: కాబట్టి uniform field లో ఏ bent లేదా curved wire కైనా, కేవలం దాని రెండు ends ను కలిపే straight line గీయండి.

misconception_watch.belief: For a bent wire you must integrate the force over every little segment and add them all up.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: In a UNIFORM field only the straight chord between the two end points matters: F = I (L_chord × B). The bends cancel exactly.

STATE_5.title: A closed loop feels zero net force (but a torque) — the chord closes

scene_composition.text: What about a closed loop?

Opposite sides carry current
in opposite directions,
so their forces point opposite ways.

The chord from start back to start
is zero → net force = 0.
The forces TWIST it instead.
A closed loop's chord closes on itself, so the net force is exactly zero — but the opposite forces form a couple that turns the loop. That torque is the next concept.

tts_sentences.text_en: Now bend the wire all the way into a closed square loop carrying current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: अब wire को पूरी तरह एक बंद square loop में मोड़िए जो current I ले जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఇప్పుడు wire ని పూర్తిగా current I తీసుకెళ్ళే ఒక closed square loop లో వంచండి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Use the chord rule. The chord runs from the starting point back to the same starting point.
tts_sentences.text_hi: chord rule इस्तेमाल करिए। chord starting point से उसी starting point तक वापस जाती है।
tts_sentences.text_te: chord rule వాడండి. chord starting point నుండి అదే starting point వరకు తిరిగి వెళ్తుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: That chord has zero length, so the net force on a closed loop in a uniform field is exactly zero.
tts_sentences.text_hi: उस chord की length zero है, इसलिए uniform field में closed loop पर net force बिल्कुल zero है।
tts_sentences.text_te: ఆ chord యొక్క length zero, కాబట్టి uniform field లో closed loop పై net force సరిగ్గా zero అవుతుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: But look at the sides. Opposite sides carry current opposite ways, so they feel forces in opposite directions.
tts_sentences.text_hi: लेकिन sides देखिए। Opposite sides विपरीत directions में current ले जाती हैं, इसलिए वे opposite directions में forces feel करती हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: కానీ sides చూడండి. Opposite sides వ్యతిరేక directions లో current తీసుకెళ్తాయి, కాబట్టి అవి opposite directions లో forces అనుభవిస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Equal and opposite forces on opposite sides do not push the loop — they twist it. That twist is a torque, and it is exactly how a motor loop spins. That is our next concept.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Opposite sides पर equal और opposite forces loop को push नहीं करतीं — वे इसे twist करती हैं। वही twist है torque, और उसी से motor loop spin होती है। यह हमारा अगला concept है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Opposite sides పై equal మరియు opposite forces loop ని push చేయవు — అవి దాన్ని twist చేస్తాయి. ఆ twist నే torque అంటారు, మరియు దాని వల్లనే motor loop spin అవుతుంది. ఇది మన తదుపరి concept.

misconception_watch.belief: A current loop in a magnetic field gets pushed along by a net force.
misconception_watch.one_line_fix: In a uniform field the net force on a closed loop is zero (the chord closes). Opposite sides feel opposite forces, which produce a TORQUE, not a push.

STATE_6.title: Try it — drag the sliders, flip the current, watch F

scene_composition.text: You drive it now

Top-right sliders: I · L · B · θ
plus a button to flip the current.
The F readout updates live.

Try: θ = 90 → biggest force
θ = 0 → no force
Flip current → F reverses
Double I → F doubles
Same right-hand rule everywhere. F = B I L sin θ — current, length, field, and the L–B angle are the four knobs.

tts_sentences.text_en: Your turn. The top-right sliders set the current I, the length L, the field B, and the angle theta.
tts_sentences.text_hi: आपकी बारी। Top-right sliders current I, length L, field B, और angle theta set करते हैं।
tts_sentences.text_te: మీ వంతు. Top-right sliders current I, length L, field B, మరియు angle theta set చేస్తాయి.

tts_sentences.text_en: Set theta to ninety degrees and the force is at its largest, B I L.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Theta को ninety degrees पर set करिए और force अपने maximum B I L पर होगी।
tts_sentences.text_te: Theta ని ninety degrees కి set చేయండి మరియు force దాని maximum B I L వద్ద ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Drag theta down to zero and the force vanishes — the wire is now along the field.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Theta को zero तक drag करिए और force गायब हो जाएगी — wire अब field के साथ है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Theta ని zero వరకు drag చేయండి మరియు force అదృశ్యమవుతుంది — wire ఇప్పుడు field వెంట ఉంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Double the current and watch the force double. The force F is straight-line proportional to the current I.
tts_sentences.text_hi: Current को double करिए और force को double होते देखिए। force F, current I के straight-line proportional है।
tts_sentences.text_te: Current ని double చేయండి మరియు force double అవడం చూడండి. force F, current I కి straight-line proportional గా ఉంటుంది.

tts_sentences.text_en: Tap the flip-current button and watch the force reverse. That is the whole concept in your hands.
tts_sentences.text_hi: flip-current button tap करिए और force को reverse होते देखिए। पूरा concept आपके हाथों में है।
tts_sentences.text_te: flip-current button tap చేయండి మరియు force reverse అవడం చూడండి. మొత్తం concept మీ చేతుల్లో ఉంది.

STATE_1.label: The push — F = I L × B; right-hand rule; current flips → F flips

STATE_2.label: Derivation as a picture — n·A·L carriers, each q·v_d·B, stacking into I·L·B

STATE_3.label: Angle trap — θ is ∠(L,B); drag θ and F falls as sin θ

STATE_4.label: Bent wire = straight chord — F = I (L_chord × B); snapshot swap (no morph)

STATE_5.label: Closed loop — chord closes → net F = 0; opposite sides → torque

STATE_6.label: Interactive — sliders for I, L, B, θ + flip-current button
STATE_6.formula_overlay:
F = B I L sin θ
F_max = B I L
flip current → F reverses
